title: Doenjang Jjigae Soybean Paste
description: Doenjang Jjigae is a quintessential Korean comfort food, a hearty and deeply savory stew bursting with umami. Its rich, earthy soybean paste base harmonizes beautifully with tender vegetables, silky tofu, and often a touch of meat or seafood, creating a warm and satisfying experience that is beloved across Korea.

ingredients.notes: adjust to taste and saltiness of doenjang

ingredients.item: Green chili pepper (e.g., Cheongyang gochu or jalapeño)
ingredients.notes: sliced, adjust to preferred spice level
sliced, for garnish and slight heat
thinly sliced, optional but recommended for richer flavor

instructions.title: Prepare the Anchovy-Kelp Broth (Myeolchi-Dashima Yuksu)
instructions.text: In a pot, combine 5 cups of water with the dried anchovies and dried kelp. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes. Remove the anchovies and kelp from the broth and discard. This flavorful broth is the foundation of an authentic doenjang jjigae.

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients and Seasoning Paste
instructions.text: While the broth simmers, prepare all your vegetables: dice the onion, slice the zucchini into half-moons, dice the potato, cube the tofu, mince the garlic, and slice the chili peppers. If using beef or pork, cut into bite-sized pieces. In a small bowl, combine the doenjang, minced garlic, and gochugaru (if using) with a tablespoon or two of the prepared anchovy broth to make a smooth paste.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics and Meat (Optional)
instructions.text: If using beef or pork, heat a small amount of oil in a dolsot (Korean earthenware pot) or a medium pot over medium heat. Add the thinly sliced beef/pork and sauté until lightly browned. Add the diced onion and continue to sauté until translucent, about 3-5 minutes. This step adds depth and richness.

instructions.title: Combine Doenjang and Broth
instructions.text: Add the prepared doenjang paste to the pot with the sautéed aromatics and meat (or directly to the pot if not using meat). Stir well to dissolve the paste, allowing it to cook for a minute to release its full aroma. Pour in the anchovy-kelp broth, stir, and bring to a gentle boil.

instructions.title: Add Vegetables and Simmer
instructions.text: Once the broth is boiling, add the diced potatoes first, as they take longer to cook. Simmer for about 5-7 minutes until they are slightly tender. Then, add the zucchini and continue to simmer for another 3-5 minutes. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed; add a little more doenjang if you prefer a stronger flavor.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Finally, add the cubed tofu, sliced green and red chili peppers, and enoki mushrooms (if using). Simmer for just 2-3 more minutes until the tofu is heated through and the mushrooms are tender. Do not overcook. Garnish with chopped scallions just before serving. Serve immediately, bubbling hot, with a bowl of steamed rice and various banchan (side dishes).

tips: Always start with a good quality doenjang. Traditional, artisanal doenjang often has a deeper, more complex flavor than mass-produced varieties.
The anchovy-kelp broth is crucial. Don't skip it or substitute with plain water, as it provides the essential umami depth.
Doenjang is already salty; taste the broth before adding more seasoning. The saltiness can vary significantly between brands.
For a spicier version, increase the amount of gochugaru or use more Cheongyang gochu (Korean hot peppers).
Serve Doenjang Jjigae in a dolsot (earthenware pot) if possible. It keeps the stew hot longer and adds to the authentic dining experience.
article:
## History of Doenjang Jjigae Soybean Paste

Doenjang, the fermented soybean paste that forms the heart of Doenjang Jjigae, is one of Korea's oldest and most fundamental condiments, with a history stretching back over a thousand years. Its origins are intertwined with the ancient practice of fermenting soybeans, a method developed to preserve food and enhance flavor. Doenjang Jjigae itself emerged as a staple dish, born out of the necessity and ingenuity of Korean households to create a nourishing and flavorful meal from readily available ingredients. It has long been a symbol of home cooking and comfort, often served daily alongside rice and other banchan, reflecting its deep integration into the Korean diet and culture. The communal act of sharing a bubbling pot of jjigae reinforces its status as a beloved national dish.

## Why This Recipe Works

This recipe for Doenjang Jjigae works by leveraging the profound umami of doenjang and the rich depth of a properly made anchovy-kelp broth. The slow simmering process allows the complex flavors of the fermented soybean paste to meld with the natural sweetness of the vegetables and the savory notes of the optional beef, creating a harmonious and deeply satisfying stew. The layering of ingredients—sautéing aromatics first, then adding harder vegetables before softer ones—ensures everything cooks perfectly without becoming mushy. The subtle kick from gochugaru and fresh chilies brightens the rich base, preventing it from being one-dimensional, while the tofu adds a silky texture that contrasts beautifully with the other ingredients.

## Chef Tips for Perfection

To truly elevate your Doenjang Jjigae, pay close attention to the quality of your doenjang; a artisanal, aged paste will yield far superior flavor. Always start with a robust anchovy-kelp broth; this is non-negotiable for authentic depth. When preparing the doenjang paste, combine it with minced garlic and a little gochugaru and bloom it slightly in the pot before adding the broth—this step enhances its fragrance and flavor. Don't overcrowd your dolsot (earthenware pot); ensure there's enough space for ingredients to simmer evenly. Finally, adjust the saltiness at the very end, as doenjang varies greatly in sodium content, and the stew will reduce slightly as it cooks.

jsonLd.name: Doenjang Jjigae Soybean Paste
jsonLd.description: Doenjang Jjigae is a quintessential Korean comfort food, a hearty and deeply savory stew bursting with umami. Its rich, earthy soybean paste base harmonizes beautifully with tender vegetables, silky tofu, and often a touch of meat or seafood, creating a warm and satisfying experience that is beloved across Korea.
jsonLd.keywords: Korean, Korean, authentic, traditional, soups, Korean, authentic, traditional, soups, stew, doenjang, comfort food
recipeIngredient: 10-12 Dried anchovies (large, for broth) (head and guts removed)
3-4 tbsp Doenjang (Korean soybean paste) (adjust to taste and saltiness of doenjang)
1 tsp Gochugaru (Korean chili flakes) (optional, for a slight kick)
1/2 block (7-8 oz) Firm or medium-firm tofu (cubed)
1 Green chili pepper (e.g., Cheongyang gochu or jalapeño) (sliced, adjust to preferred spice level)
1/2 Red chili pepper (sliced, for garnish and slight heat)
4 oz (100g) Beef brisket (chadolbagi) or pork belly (thinly sliced, optional but recommended for richer flavor)

recipeInstructions.name: Prepare the Anchovy-Kelp Broth (Myeolchi-Dashima Yuksu)
